title: Has anyone seen this Title screens with furigana
display_title: Has anyone seen this Title screens with furigana
wikitext:
[]: Has anyone seen this? (Title screens with furigana)             Quote Post by svenge » Mon Aug 27, 2012 5:37 am It's widely known that during Eva's initial TV broadcast the Japanese title screens for each episode had furigana (aka "ruby text') placed alongside the kanji, but I'd never actually seen them as such before. Anyhow, I was poking around a Chinese-language Eva blog , when I stumbled upon this image: SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by ReiAyanami25 » Mon Aug 27, 2012 6:16 am Very interesting. As you have stated, I expect that perhaps the broadcaster added the Furigana to make it more accessable, for some reason. However, the Furigana is used for the whole title, it appears, not an individual Kanji, which I find odd. Furigana, I have always assumed, is an aid for children that cannot read more complicated Kanji that have not been taught to them in school yet. I certainly found Furigana useful back when I used to read Japanese children's books for practise, at the age of 11. Nowadays I can read plenty of books without confusion, as well as newspapers. What interests me with the titles in question is that they all have Furigana, and I doubt that Anno and Gainax would have intended for children to view NGE. I expect that Gainax did not want them, because I must admit that myself, the fact I can read titles without the aid of Furigana is a useful one, and when the needed Furigana is present, it can spoil the visual consistency and even appearance of the text. I expect that it was a similar case with Gainax and Anno, but the Furigana was forced into it by the broadcaster, perhaps to comply with usage found elsewhere on the channels. Perhaps NGE was shown at a time when children would be watching.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Aug 27, 2012 11:25 am Wasn't Eva in Japan originally broadcast before or after Ninja Turtles or Power Rangers or some children's TV show? I remember reading that Eva didn't do well in its initial broadcast but a later late night broadcast helped make the series a success and paved the way for midnight anime in Japan. Interesting that the episode titles had furigana at one point. I know Sadamoto's NGE manga includes furigana.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Mon Aug 27, 2012 11:40 am Sailor Star Dust wrote: I remember reading that Eva didn't do well in its initial broadcast but a later late night broadcast helped make the series a success and paved the way for midnight anime in Japan.

[]:             Quote Post by JoeD80 » Mon Aug 27, 2012 4:56 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: Wasn't Eva in Japan originally broadcast before or after Ninja Turtles or Power Rangers or some children's TV show?
